title: Yosys error message does not provide information about the location of the error.
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.61+112 (git sha1 106f289e3-dirty, aarch64-apple-darwin23.5-clang++ 18.1.8 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

macOS

### Reproduction Steps

Run the yosys.sh script in the sample project below.

[work-toggle.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24975579/work-toggle.zip)

It runs the command

```
yosys -p 'synth_gowin -top main' -q main.v
```

main.v
```
module main (
    input CLK,
    input RESET,
    output reg OUT
);

  always @(posedge CLK or posedge RESET) begin
    OUT <= ~OUT;
  end

endmodule
```

### Expected Behavior

Yosys provides some information that helps locating the errors. E.g. file name, line number, module name, signal name, etc.

Note that `verilator_bin --lint-only ...` doesn't catch the problem so Yosys is the only source for error location.



### Actual Behavior

Yosys prints an error message that doesn't help locating the error.

```
⦗OSS CAD Suite⦘ ~/work-toggle$ ./yosys.sh 
+ yosys --version
Yosys 0.61+112 (git sha1 106f289e3-dirty, aarch64-apple-darwin23.5-clang++ 18.1.8 -fPIC -O3)
+ yosys -p 'synth_gowin -top main' -q main.v
ERROR: Multiple edge sensitive events found for this signal!
⦗OSS CAD Suite⦘ ~/work-toggle$ 
```
